subject: [PATCH v3] mm/huge_memory: unlock i_mmap_rwsem before releasing after-split folios

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: The reviewer pointed out that the patch may have introduced a pre-existing bug, referencing an external link to Sashiko, which appears to be analyzing the patchset.
sentiment_signals: raised a question about potential existing issue
reviews.raw_body:
Sashiko might have found a pre-existing bug in there:

	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260716095424.471052-1-kirill@shutemov.name

(why is mapping_set_update() a) a macro and b) undocumented?)

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug where the i_mmap_rwsem lock is not properly released before freeing after-split folios in the huge_memory module. The issue arises when the split relies on the @folio staying locked and in the page cache, but the unlock loop unlocks @folio before releasing the i_mmap_rwsem lock. This can lead to a concurrent final iput() evicting and RCU-freeing the inode before the i_mmap_unlock_read() function touches the i_mmap_rwsem lock, resulting in a slab-use-after-free error. The patch resolves this by unlocking i_mmap_rwsem right after remap_page(), before the loop that unlocks and frees the after-split folios.

reviews.author: Miaohe Lin
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not address the issue of concurrent final iput() evicting and RCU-freeing the inode before i_mmap_unlock_read() touches i_mmap_rwsem, which can lead to a slab-use-after-free bug.
sentiment_signals: concurrent final iput() evicting and RCU-freeing the inode

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: Matthew Wilcox noted that the commit message is unrelated to the original patch and suggested taking it up with a different person.
reviews.raw_body:
commit 62e72d2cf702

    So make madvise(..., MADV_COLLAPSE) also call xas_set_lru() to pass the
    list_lru which we may want to insert xa_node into later.  And move
    mapping_set_update to mm/internal.h, and turn into a macro to avoid
    including extra headers in mm/internal.h.

Take it up with Kairui.  And whoever it was added that patch to
linux-mm.

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer Kairui Song suggested moving the i_mmap_unlock_read() call before the after-split unlock loop and making it a function, but initially wanted to make it an inline helper in pagemap.h or internal.h
reviews.raw_body:
Right, I initially wanted to make it a inline helper, since this function
were a macro and later become a static helper, so it should be super cheap
in the hot path. That requires to include extra headers in internal.h
and that looked a bit more uglier and every internel.h user will have
extra header dependency. Moving it to pagemap.h also looked confusing
along size other mapping_set_* helper, they are completely different
things, and this help should stay in mm/.

So I just partially restored how it was before b64e74e95aa6, It was
never documented though.

We can make it a function for sure, open to suggestions on this.

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug where the i_mmap_rwsem lock is not properly released before freeing after-split folios in the huge_memory module. The issue arises when the split relies on the @folio staying locked and in the page cache, but the unlock loop unlocks @folio before releasing the i_mmap_rwsem lock. This can lead to a concurrent final iput() evicting and RCU-freeing the inode before the i_mmap_unlock_read() function touches the i_mmap_rwsem lock, resulting in a slab-use-after-free error. The patch resolves this by unlocking i_mmap_rwsem right after remap_page(), before the loop that unlocks and frees the after-split folios.
